story: Herb Score was on fire in 1955! The Cleveland Indians pitcher blazed through batters, setting a new rookie record. By September 14, he had racked up 235 strikeouts. story: Snowflakes in summer? It happened in Red Lodge, Montana! On September 14, 1982, a freak storm blanketed the town with a whopping 36 inches of snow. story: Winds howled and waves crashed as Hurricane Florence slammed into Wrightsville Beach, North Carolina on September 14, 2018. title: Submarine Breaks New Ground
story: The USS New Jersey made waves in 2024 as the US Navy's first submarine with both men and women crew members. It marked a big step forward for equality in the Navy's underwater forces.

title: Handel's Musical Marathon
story: Imagine composing a masterpiece in just 24 days! That's exactly what George Frideric Handel did on September 14, 1741. The famous composer finished his 'Messiah' oratorio after working nonstop for over three weeks. title: Explorer's Daring Journey Through Africa
story: Brave explorer Henry Morton Stanley set off on a thrilling adventure in 1876. On September 14, his expedition left Rwanda, venturing into the heart of Africa.
